findings.claim: 此改动触及 correctness-forces-slow-path（kernel-efficiency），该类问题历史上主要在 [any (NCCL all-reduce path); TE attention path affected, tensor parallel (and pipeline parallel forward_step), tensor-parallel (async-TP, symmetric memory fused matmul+reduce-scatter)] 配置下显现；The change reorders an early-return guard in split_decodes_prefills_and_extends so the fast decode-only path (max_query_len <= decode_threshold) is taken before

This diff FIXES (does not introduce) a slow-kernel-gate regression for static/weight actorder WNA16 Marlin MoE under TP. In the parent, `self.is_k_full = (not self.actorder) or (part == full)` treated ANY truthy actorder value (including 'static'/'weight'/ActivationOrdering enum) as act-order, so under TP (part != full) it set is_k_full=False. That flag flows through make_wna16_moe_kernel -> MarlinExperts -> the marlin_moe_wna16 CUDA kernel, where `cache_scales_chunk = has_act_order && !is_k_full` and `sh_g_idx_size = has_act_order && !is_k_full ? stages*tb_k/4 : 0`, i.e. is_k_full=False forces the slower runtime activation-reorder (g_idx) path with extra shared memory. But for static/weight actorder, weights are reordered at quant time and g_idx is emptied in _process_weights_marlin, so the full-K fast path (is_k_full=True, has_act_order disabled) is correct. The new _w2_scale_sharding sets is_k_full=True for actorder in {'static','weight',None}, restoring the fast kernel path. This is a legitimate perf fix, not a regression.
findings.suggested_benchmark: WNA16 (int4 g128/g32) compressed-tensors MoE with actorder='static' under TP>1: compare marlin_moe latency/throughput before vs after (is_k_full False->True).

findings.claim: Also note load_full_w2 previously loaded the full (unsharded) w2 scales for static/weight actorder (self.actorder truthy). That is a one-time setup/memory concern rather than hot-path, but the new gating (load_full_w2 only for actorder=='group') additionally reduces per-rank w2 scale memory for static actorder. No hot-path regression introduced.

此改动触及 suboptimal-kernel-gate（kernel-efficiency），该类问题历史上主要在 [ZeRO-Offload / CPU-Adam, Megatron-FSDP with gradient_accumulation_fusion enabled, TP1/PP1 in test config] 配置下显现；This feature enables MXFP4 MoE on ROCm by routing to a specific backend and adds a new activation path. The key perf-relevant change is in select_mxfp4_moe_back

In `run_output_dispatcher_loop`, whenever a RequestBatch carries `scheduler_stats` (i.e. essentially every engine step / output batch — the hottest path in the frontend), the new code unconditionally calls `inner.lora_adapter_states()`, which acquires the request-registry lock and iterates over ALL currently tracked in-flight requests, allocating two fresh `BTreeSet<String>` and cloning `adapter_name` for every LoRA request. This work is performed on every step even in the common case where LoRA is not used at all: the scan/lock/allocation happens regardless, and only the final `filter_map(|t| t.lora.as_ref())` skips non-LoRA entries — but the full `self.requests.values()` iteration + set construction + lock acquisition still runs each step. For a replica serving many concurrent requests, this adds a per-step O(N_inflight) pass plus heap allocations that scale with concurrency, on the critical output-dispatch loop that previously did no such scan.
findings.suggested_benchmark: Steady-state token-generation throughput / per-step dispatch latency with a high number of concurrent non-LoRA requests (e.g. 512+ in-flight), comparing before/after this commit to isolate the per-step lora_adapter_states() scan cost.

findings.claim: `RequestRegistry::lora_adapter_states()` builds `(running, waiting)` sets by cloning every LoRA adapter name string into a BTreeSet on every invocation, and it is invoked once per scheduler-stats-bearing output batch. With many concurrent LoRA requests this is an O(N_lora) allocate-and-clone per step; combined with the per-step invocation this is repeated allocation churn proportional to in-flight LoRA request count. Consider caching/diffing the derived sets or only recomputing when a lifecycle event actually changed a request's phase, rather than rebuilding both sets from scratch each step.
findings.suggested_benchmark: Micro-benchmark lora_adapter_states() over a registry of N=1000 LoRA-tagged requests to quantify per-call allocation cost.

findings.claim: The default value of `gather_actual_addresses(timeout=...)` is changed from a literal `60.0` to `envs.VLLM_ENGINE_READY_TIMEOUT_S`. In vllm/envs.py that env var defaults to 600 (10 minutes), i.e. 10x the previous 60s. This is precisely the 'static config/default value chooses a slower path' mechanism: on multi-API-server startup failure (a child never reports its bound ZMQ address), the parent will now block on `connection.wait` for up to 600s before raising RuntimeError, instead of failing fast at 60s. Startup-hang symptoms therefore get 10x worse. This is the intended bug fix (previous 60s was too short for some legitimately slow startups), but reviewers should confirm 600s is acceptable as a failure-detection ceiling, since it dramatically lengthens the time-to-error on genuine startup failures.
findings.suggested_benchmark: Measure time-to-failure of APIServerProcessManager.gather_actual_addresses when a child process is killed/hangs before reporting its address, comparing old 60s vs new 600s default.

findings.claim: Using `timeout: float = envs.VLLM_ENGINE_READY_TIMEOUT_S` as a default argument value binds the env var at function-definition (import) time, not at call time. vllm/envs.py implements lazy env resolution via module __getattr__, but writing `envs.VLLM_ENGINE_READY_TIMEOUT_S` in a default arg forces the getattr to execute once when the module/class body is evaluated, freezing the value. Any later os.environ mutation of VLLM_ENGINE_READY_TIMEOUT_S (e.g. tests using monkeypatch.setenv, as in tests/v1/engine/test_engine_core_client.py) will NOT be reflected in this default. This is a correctness/config-plumbing smell rather than a hot-path perf issue, but it undermines the very intent of making the timeout configurable.

此改动触及 correctness-forces-slow-path（kernel-efficiency），该类问题历史上主要在 [any (NCCL all-reduce path); TE attention path affected, tensor parallel (and pipeline parallel forward_step), tensor-parallel (async-TP, symmetric memory fused matmul+reduce-scatter)] 配置下显现；To satisfy Marlin's MIN_THREAD_N=64 constraint under TP>=2 for the non-interleaved Qwen3.5 layout, this replicates the ba_proj across ranks instead of TP-shardi

get_computed_blocks() runs on the per-request scheduler hot path. The diff adds a new block that, when enable_kv_cache_events and kv_cache_report_mode=='full', iterates over every KV cache group and calls block_pool.emit_cached_block_events(). That function re-walks all num_cached_blocks: for each cached block it calls maybe_convert_block_hash() and generate_block_hash_extra_keys() (per-block work), building cached_hashes and extra_keys_list of length O(num_cached_blocks), then constructs a full BlockStored event (which itself slices request.all_token_ids[0:num_cached_blocks*block_size], copying every prefix token). This is O(num_prefix_tokens) work re-derived on the scheduling path for every request that gets a prefix-cache hit — precisely the 'repeat work every scheduling iteration' hot-path pattern. It is gated to full report mode so only affects users who enable it, but for them long-prefix reused requests pay recomputation + a large token_ids copy every time they are (re)scheduled.
findings.suggested_benchmark: Prefix-cache-heavy workload (long shared system prompt, many requests) with enable_kv_cache_events=True and kv_cache_report_mode='full'; measure scheduler get_computed_blocks latency and per-request token_ids copy overhead vs incremental mode.

findings.claim: In both cache_full_blocks and the new emit_cached_block_events, the BlockStored event is built via _build_block_stored_event which does request.all_token_ids[start_token_idx:end_token_idx]. For emit_cached_block_events start_token_idx=0 and end_token_idx=num_cached_blocks*block_size, so the entire cached prefix's token ids are copied into a new list per event on the scheduler path. For large reused prefixes this is a sizable per-request allocation/copy that only feeds an external event consumer.
findings.suggested_benchmark: Compare event-emission cost with short vs very long reused prefixes under full report mode.
